summary: Decompiled most of the common AirCatch/tether-catch action into a new ftCo_AirCatch.c, moving 817 lines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s and wiring the new object into obj_files.mk. The PR report says the file reached 95.98% overall: many small callbacks matched 100%, ftCo_AirCatch_Anim reached 98.25%, ftCo_800C3CC0 reached 98.49%, and ftCo_800C3A14 remained weak at 68.03%. The work also added the dedicated ftCo_AirCatch.h header, common motion vars for aircatch/aircatchhit, and enough Link/Samus item, dat-attr, collision, and mplib prototypes to express Link/Young Link hookshot and Samus grapple behavior in C.
what_changed: Added new source file src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AirCatch.c with implementations for ftCo_800C3A14, ftCo_800C3B10, ftCo_800C3BE8, ftCo_800C3CC0, ftCo_AirCatch_Anim, AirCatch/AirCatchHit IASA stubs, AirCatch/AirCatchHit physics, and AirCatch collision.
Removed the corresponding AirCatch assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s and added ftCo_AirCatch.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk while leaving the remaining ft_0C31 assembly object present.
Added ftCo_AirCatch.h with function prototypes and inferred m2c stack helper structs for ftCo_800C3A14 and ftCo_AirCatch_Anim.

Moved AirCatch declarations out of src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h into the new ftCommon AirCatch header, then included that header from several common fighter action files and Link/Young Link/Samus init files.
Extended ftCommon_MotionVars with co.aircatch.x0 at fp+2340 and co.aircatchhit fields x0/x4 at fp+2340/fp+2344.

Typed Link/Young Link tether-related fields and dat attributes: ftLk_FighterVars.xC became Item_GObj*, and ftLk_DatAttrs gained explicit xA4/xA8/xAC/xB0/xBC-style fields used by AirCatch timing and hookshot creation.
Typed Samus grapple-related fields and dat attributes: ftSamus_FighterVars.x223C became Item_GObj*, and ftSs_DatAttrs gained explicit offsets through xD0 including xBC/xC0/xC4/xC8 timing fields.

Added item prototypes for Link hookshot functions it_802A2BA4, it_802A2B10, it_802A78B8, it_802A7AF0, it_802A7AAC, it_802A7B34 and Samus grapple functions it_802B7C18, it_802B7B84, it_802BAA58, it_802BAAE4, it_802BAC80, it_802BAC3C, it_802BACC4.

Improved collision/map prototypes used by the decomp: mpColl_80044164 and mpColl_800443C4 now take CollData* and ledge-id pointers and return bool; mpLib_800524DC was prototyped as a bool line/intersection-style check with coordinate arguments.
Added MPCOLL_FLAGS_B25 to common_structs.h, complementing existing B24 use in ftCo_800C3A14.
smart_moves: Separated the AirCatch unit into its own ftCommon header/source rather than leaving prototypes in the generic ft_0C31.h bucket, making future action-specific includes easier to reason about.
Kept hard-to-match functions guarded with ASM plus C bodies under the existing MUST_MATCH/WIP pattern, preserving exact assembly fallback while documenting the intended C logic.

Used fighter-kind branching to share one common AirCatch implementation across Link, Young Link, and Samus while selecting the correct motion states, tether item constructors, callbacks, timing fields, and cleanup functions.

Promoted unknown item callback and fighter-var fields to Item_GObj* and function-pointer-compatible prototypes only where the AirCatch implementation needed them, improving type information without broad unrelated rewrites.
Modeled AirCatch frame timing through dat_attrs offsets, e.g. Link xA4/xA8/xB0 and Samus xBC/xC0/xC4/xC8, which made the long animation callback clearer and localized character-specific timing data.
Preserved matching-sensitive artifacts such as literal constants, Vec3 literals, SDATA assertion strings, unused-stack comments, M2C_FIELD accesses, and explicit S32_TO_F32 conversion constants.
decomp_lessons: For common fighter actions that are implemented differently per character, first identify the shared action state and then split character-specific behavior by Fighter kind and dat_attrs layout rather than duplicating whole callbacks.

Tether-style actions require coordinated fighter, item, and collision typing: the fighter action owns accessory/death callbacks, the item object stores article data, and map collision checks can abort the tether and fall back to ftCo_800968C8.
When converting an asm slice to C, adding a dedicated header can reduce dependency pressure: dependent fighter actions include ftCo_AirCatch.h instead of the broad ft_0C31.h placeholder header.
Motion-var unions should name fields by action once an action is understood; here co.aircatch and co.aircatchhit replaced raw fp+2340/fp+2344 access in the new code.
Use exact function-pointer callback types for item accessory/death callbacks as soon as they are assigned into Fighter fields; this prevents cascading UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS usage in action code.
Matching-sensitive float/int comparisons against animation counters often need explicit int dat_attr fields plus S32_TO_F32-style conversion constants to reproduce compiler behavior.
naming_conventions: New common action files use the ftCo_AirCatch.c / ftCo_AirCatch.h naming pattern under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon.
Common fighter action callbacks follow the ftCo_AirCatch_Anim, ftCo_AirCatch_IASA, ftCo_AirCatch_Phys, ftCo_AirCatch_Coll, ftCo_AirCatchHit_* naming style.
Still-unknown helper functions retained address-based names such as ftCo_800C3A14, ftCo_800C3B10, ftCo_800C3BE8, and ftCo_800C3CC0.
Character-specific motion-state names used existing-style constants such as ftLk_MS_AirCatch, ftLk_MS_AirCatchHit, ftSs_MS_AirCatch, and ftSs_MS_AirCatchHit.
New per-action motion vars were named under fp->mv.co.aircatch and fp->mv.co.aircatchhit instead of generic x2340-style access.
Typed but semantically uncertain dat attribute members kept offset names like xA4, xA8, xB0, xBC, xC0, xC4, and xC8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The file report documents 95.98% overall matching, with 100% for ftCo_800C3B10, ftCo_800C3BE8, ftCo_AirCatch_Phys, ftCo_AirCatch_Coll, ftCo_AirCatchHit_Phys, and three empty IASA/Anim stubs.
Several functions were written as ASM functions with C implementations enabled outside MUST_MATCH or under WIP, and inline nofralloc assembly retained for strict matching fallback.
ftCo_AirCatch_Anim used explicit goto exit control flow to consolidate the final ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining check and ftCo_800968C8 fallback, matching the assembly’s shared exit structure.
M2C_FIELD was used for article-data and dat-attribute accesses where the precise struct type was not fully established, avoiding premature or unsafe struct definitions.
Unused stack padding comments and conditional dummy stack arrays were retained in matching-sensitive functions to account for stack layout differences.

Literal constants were declared with exact storage and type choices, including double literals for 5, 0, 1, 8, 2, 0.2, float literals for 0, 1, 20, Vec3 constants, and SDATA strings for HSD_JObj assertion paths.

ftCo_800C3A14 was the weakest match at 68.03%, likely because it reconstructs a CollData stack copy and ledge-collision checks from a large raw memory-copy pattern; this is evidence of a remaining matching hotspot, not a fully solved tactic.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice, so there is no direct reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that improve ftCo_800C3A14 above 68.03% or remove its inline assembly fallback.
Search for later renames of ftLk_DatAttrs xA4/xA8/xAC/xB0/xBC and ftSs_DatAttrs xBC/xC0/xC4/xC8 to semantic tether timing names.
Check whether the possible typo/cross-header declaration of it_802A77DC in itsamusgrapple.h was later moved or corrected.
Find later work that replaces M2C_FIELD accesses in ftCo_AirCatch_Anim with typed hookshot/grapple article-data structs.
Search for additional AirCatch/tether review or cleanup PRs touching ftCo_AirCatch.h, itlinkhookshot.h, itsamusgrapple.h, or mpLib_800524DC.
